title: IntentNormalizerOut
description:
Schema for the JSON object returned by the unified intent normalizer prompt (prompts/intent_normalizer_prompt.md). Mirrors `IntentNormalizerOut` in crates/clawd/src/intent_router.rs and is enforced by the `intent_normalizer_schema_drift` unit test (any drift between this file and the parser fails the build).

§3.5c-小切口: this file is the reference template for schema-driven prompt I/O. Future schema-driven parsers should follow the same pattern — author the JSON Schema next to the prompt under `prompts/schemas/`, then add a drift test that asserts (a) the parser struct mirrors `properties`, and (b) every declared `enum` value parses to a non-default variant in the corresponding `parse_*` function.

resolved_user_intent.description: Single-sentence resolved intent. Empty string allowed only when needs_clarify=true.

answer_candidate.description: Optional exact scalar answer candidate recovered from recent turns or memory. Use empty string when no direct answer is available.

resume_behavior.description: Whether the message resumes a paused task. `parse_resume_behavior` accepts these tokens (case-insensitive); anything else maps to `none`.

schedule_kind.description: Optional schedule operation classification. `parse_schedule_kind` accepts these operation tokens. Frequency/type tokens such as `daily`, `weekly`, `interval`, `once`, and `cron` belong in `schedule_intent.schedule.type`, not here.

needs_clarify.description: True when the resolved intent is missing a key target/scope and the message is otherwise executable.

clarify_question.description: Free text shown to user when needs_clarify=true. Should be empty when needs_clarify=false.

reason.description: Short routing rationale used for audit/journal logs.

decision.description: Initial machine hint for the legacy first-layer boundary. Planner-loop decision envelopes are the semantic authority when agent-loop routing is enabled.

schedule_intent.description: Optional structured schedule output. Schema deferred; parsed by claw-core schedule_intent decoder.

output_contract.description: Output contract used by finalize layers. The prompt must emit this object; parser recovery still accepts null/missing values and normalizes them to defaults.

scalar_count_filter.description: Optional machine filter for filesystem scalar-count contracts. Use null when not applicable. target_kind: any=file+dir direct entries, file=files only, dir=directories only. extensions is a list of language-neutral file-extension tokens without dots, such as md/json/log/toml.

list_selector.description: Optional machine selector for filesystem and archive listing contracts. Use null when not applicable. target_kind constrains object class; limit is a positive bounded result count; sort_by is a stable machine sort token; include_metadata means observed metadata must be available for final output; include_hidden controls whether dot-prefixed entries are included in directory inventory.

execution_recipe.description: Optional ops/closed-loop hint. `kind=none` means normalizer explicitly classified the request as NOT an ops loop (B1 fix); `null` means normalizer abstained.

turn_type.description: Optional turn taxonomy classification for task-state patching and run-control handling.

target_task_policy.description: Optional policy describing how this turn should attach to the current active task.

should_interrupt_active_run.description: True when this turn should interrupt a currently running execution before applying the new turn semantics.

state_patch.description: Optional structured state/task patch payload for corrections, appended constraints, approvals, temporary alias_bindings, ordered_entry_ref ({"index":N,"index_base":1} or {"relative_offset":K}), quantity_comparison ({"selection":"max"|"min","source":"recent_count_inventory"}) for direct answers over prior count_inventory observations, scalar_count_filter ({"target_kind":"any|file|dir","include_hidden":true|false,"recursive":true|false,"extensions":["ext"]}) for filesystem scalar-count object/extension/hidden/recursive filters, structured_field_selector (exact machine field/key/path for JSON/TOML/YAML field-value extraction when known, including clarify turns where the file/path locator is still missing; also accepts service-status field family selectors such as system_health.* for host/system-only health projections), runtime_async_job_start ({"command":"...","execution_mode":"async_start","async_adapter_kind":"local_process_poll","poll_after_seconds":N,"expires_in_seconds":N}) for explicit async/background local command starts, required_machine_fields ([exact agent/runtime machine field paths such as decision_envelope.control_intent when final output must include protocol/control fields; use task_lifecycle.state, task_lifecycle.can_poll, task_lifecycle.can_cancel, and task_lifecycle.checkpoint_id for task runtime/lifecycle/status-field observations]), required_content_literals ([exact current-turn replacement/added content literals that must visibly remain in an active-task rewrite; not output-control wording]), forbidden_visible_literals ([exact current-turn old/rejected content literals to omit from an active-task rewrite]), replacement_pairs ([{"from":"old literal","to":"new literal"}]), primary_task_update="replace" with active_task_boundary="new_deliverable" for a standalone new deliverable that should become the editable active task, deictic_reference ({"target":"current_action_result"|"current_turn_locator"|"comparison_result"|"unresolved_prior_object"|"missing_locator"|"ambiguous_locator"}), and similar turn-level updates.

attachment_processing_required.description: True when the turn includes attachments/media that must be normalized before planner/chat handling.
